content.subtitle: Form
items: noun + be in the correct form + past participle (+ by/with + noun)

content.subtitle: Tense table
items: present simple: am/is/are + past participle - Bananas are grown in tropical areas.
present continuous: am/is/are + being + past participle - The café is being redecorated.
present perfect simple: has/have + been + past participle - Have the carrots been peeled?
past simple: was/were + past participle - The meal was served in an elegant dining room.
past continuous: was/were + being + past participle - We asked for coffee while the bill was being prepared.
past perfect simple: had + been + past participle - All the food had been eaten by the time I got there.
will future: will + be + past participle - Your pizza will be delivered in forty minutes.
be going to future: is/are going to + be + past participle - Burgerland is going to be sued by overweight customers.
future perfect simple: will + have + been + past participle - All the grapes will have been harvested by the end of September.
modal: modal + be + past participle - The chicken breast should be brushed with oil and then fried.
modal + perfect infinitive: modal + have + been + past participle - The groceries should have been delivered by now.
-ing (gerund): being + past participle - I don't like being told what to do in the kitchen.

content.subtitle: Use
items: When we don't know who does/did something: My groceries have been stolen!
When it's obvious who does/did something: A boy was arrested in town yesterday for stealing an apple.
When it's not important who does/did something: The French bistro is being knocked down.
When we want to emphasise new information or use a formal style: The potato was brought to Europe by Sir Walter Raleigh.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: We do not normally use verbs in the passive in the present perfect continuous, past perfect continuous, future continuous or future perfect continuous tenses. Instead, we use a different phrase: The restaurant has been under construction for four years.
We only normally use 'by' to say who did something when it is important information: Margarine was invented by a French chef.
We usually use 'with' when we talk about the thing used to do something: The soup should then be stirred with a spoon.
Some verbs are not normally used in the passive. They include intransitive verbs (without objects), such as appear and die, and some common transitive verbs, such as have, let, lack, etc.

content.subtitle: Form
items: noun + is/are said to + bare infinitive/perfect infinitive
It is said that + clause

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Active: People think he is a great chef. Passive: He is thought to be a great chef. / It is thought that he is a great chef.
Active: People believe he was a great chef. Passive: He is believed to have been a great chef. / It is believed that he was a great chef.

Active: People claim he has had an influence on many other chefs. Passive: He is claimed to have had an influence on many other chefs. / It is claimed that he has had an influence on many other chefs.

Active: People say he has been making the best cheese in the area for over thirty years. Passive: He is said to have been making the best cheese in the area for over thirty years. / It is said that he has been making the best cheese in the area for over thirty years.

Active: People estimated that his restaurant was worth over $10 million. Passive: His restaurant was estimated to be / to have been worth over $10 million. / It was estimated that his restaurant was worth over $10 million.
Active: People have suggested that he is a great chef. Passive: It has been suggested that he is a great chef.

content.subtitle: Form
items: noun + have/get in the correct form + noun + past participle (+ by/with + noun)

content.subtitle: Use
items: To show that someone arranges for someone else to do something for them: I have my groceries delivered by the supermarket once a week. / We are having a new cooker put in tomorrow. / We had a large wedding cake made. / Have you had your kitchen decorated? / We are going to have the food for the party made by a catering company.
To refer to an unpleasant situation which hasn't been arranged: We had our herb garden vandalised while we were away. / The Smiths have had their new microwave stolen.

content.subtitle: Notes
items: Using the verb 'get' is usually more informal than using 'have': Can you go and get this recipe photocopied for me?

We can also use 'get somebody to do' and 'have somebody do' when we want to refer to the person we arrange to do something for us: Why don't you get the chef to prepare you a vegetarian meal? / Why don't you have the chef prepare you a vegetarian meal?

content.subtitle: Verbs with two objects
items: bring, buy, get, give, lend, make, offer, owe, pass, promise, send, show, take, teach, tell, write, etc.

content.subtitle: Active
items: A friend gave my sister this cookery book. / A friend gave this cookery book to my sister.

content.subtitle: Passive
items: My sister was given this cookery book by a friend. / This cookery book was given to my sister by a friend.

sections.title: A: Passive forms
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: The prime minister was / has criticised for his recent actions.
When I walked past the Wilsons' house, their new sofa was / has being delivered.
Our teacher was / has told us to take our favourite book to school tomorrow.
I think my mobile was / has been stolen!
Jonathan was / has chosen to play the lead role in the school play.
I'm sleeping downstairs because my bedroom is being painted / has been painting.
This picture was / has probably taken during the winter.
Your essays must be / have handed in on Friday morning.
Someone was / has left their wallet on the floor.
Did you hear about the bank being / having robbed?
Treasure Island was / has written by Robert Louis Stevenson.
It was a real shock when my dad was / has fired from his job.
The Vikings had visited America before it was / has discovered by Columbus.
When we got to the airport, we learned that our flight was / had been delayed.
Was / Has your ticket for the concert tomorrow paid for by you or your parents?

sections.title: B: Passive transformation
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct passive form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: The Earth (hold) by the gravity of the Sun and orbits around it.
The first feature-length comedy film (create) by Charlie Chaplin.
The award for best video (present) later this evening.
By the time you read this, I (arrest) for murder.
I don't know whether our tests (mark) yet or not.
Radio waves (discover) by Marconi.
You wouldn't think it to look at him now, but Jack (bully) when he was at school.
Your application (consider) and we will let you know as soon as we've made a decision.
The roof of the car can (lower) by pressing this button here.
Our tent (blow) over in the night by the wind.
Chess (play) for around two thousand years now.
Two men (question) at this moment by police in connection with the burglary.

sections.title: C: Write sentences in the passive

items.sentence: Our car / service / a mechanic / at the moment.
items.answer: Our car is being serviced by a mechanic at the moment.

items.sentence: A man / shoot / an air gun / outside the petrol station last night.
items.answer: A man was shot with an air gun outside the petrol station last night.

items.sentence: Gunpowder / invent / the Chinese.
items.answer: Gunpowder was invented by the Chinese.

items.sentence: At the surgery yesterday, I / examine / Dr Peterson / and I / give / a prescription.
items.answer: At the surgery yesterday, I was examined by Dr Peterson and I was given a prescription.

items.sentence: I went to see it because I / tell / it was a good film / all my friends.
items.answer: I went to see it because I had been told it was a good film by all my friends.

items.sentence: This photograph / take / my grandfather.
items.answer: This photograph was taken by my grandfather.

items.sentence: It looked like the window / break / a hammer / some time before.
items.answer: It looked like the window had been broken with a hammer some time before.

items.sentence: Our dog / give / an injection / a special syringe / the vet.
items.answer: Our dog was given an injection with a special syringe by the vet.

items.sentence: The winning goal in last night's match / score / Donatello / a brilliant free kick.
items.answer: The winning goal in last night's match was scored by Donatello with a brilliant free kick.

items.sentence: Your cheque / send / last Friday and / should / deliver / to you tomorrow.
items.answer: Your check was sent last Friday and should be delivered to you tomorrow.

sections.title: D: Rewrite using the phrase given

items.sentence: They have been building the new road for a long time now. (under construction)
items.answer: The new road has been under construction for a long time now.

items.sentence: They had been training the horse for the race for over a year. (in training)
items.answer: The horse had been in training for the race for over a year.

items.sentence: They have been discussing the issue in Parliament. (under discussion)
items.answer: The issue has been under discussion in Parliament.

items.sentence: They had been observing the criminal for the past two weeks. (under observation)
items.answer: The criminal had been under observation for the past two weeks.

items.sentence: They have been using this plane for over 25 years now. (in use)
items.answer: This plane has been in use for over 25 years now.

items.sentence: They have been developing the Cyborg D423 robot for over ten years. (in development)
items.answer: The Cyborg D423 robot has been in development for over ten years.

sections.title: E: Impersonal passive
sections.instructions: Rewrite in the passive starting with the words given.

items.sentence: People say that Bali is a beautiful island.
items.answer: It is said that Bali is a beautiful island.

items.sentence: People generally think that life won't be found on Mars.
items.answer: It is generally thought that life won't be found on Mars.

items.sentence: It is generally said that Christmas is too commercialised.
items.answer: Christmas is generally said to be too commercialised.

items.sentence: People often argue that prison doesn't work.
items.answer: It is often argued that prison doesn't work.

items.sentence: People have suggested that the school should start to produce a magazine.
items.answer: It has been suggested that the school start to produce a magazine.

items.sentence: People say that crocodile tastes like squid.
items.answer: Crocodile is said to taste like squid.

items.sentence: It is said that the Vikings discovered America before Columbus.
items.answer: The Vikings are said to have discovered America before Columbus.

items.sentence: People think that heart disease is caused by eating the wrong things.
items.answer: Heart disease is thought to be caused by eating the wrong things.

items.sentence: We ______ a swimming pool put in this week.
Why don't you get a doctor ______ at your arm?
My teeth were a little yellow so I ______ by the dentist.
Stuart's thinking of having ______.
My sister ______ her ear pierced last weekend.
Mum and Dad didn't fancy cooking, so we got a pizza ______.
You should ______ a professional to check your house for earthquake damage.
Kelly wanted to have a live band ______ at her wedding.
Can we ______ this summer?
We ______ while we were on holiday.

sections.title: G: Causative transformation
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
Someone has scratched my car on the door! (been)
My car ______ on the door!

Tommy is having an operation right now. (being)
Tommy ______ right now.

Has Fiona invited you to her party? (been)
Have ______ party?

Shakespeare probably wrote this play in 1587. (written)
This play ______ Shakespeare in 1587.

The government is considering a new law to ban smoking following new research. (considered)
A new law to ban smoking ______ following new research.

My mum told me to go to Megagrocer's. (sent)
I ______ my mum.

I didn't understand a word so I asked my teacher and she explained it to me. (got)
I didn't understand a word so I ______ it to me.

sections.title: H: Error correction (extra words)
sections.instructions: Find the extra word in each line.

items.sentence: My wedding had been being arranged for months.
I had the caterers to give me a menu and have got them to provide me with samples...
I also wanted to have us a string quartet play classical music and I arranged that, too.
My dress was been being made specially and I had told the dressmaker put silver thread in it.
Finally, the big day got arrived.
They called me and said it had been being damaged!
I had my best friend quickly to pop out and get a new dress.
I had got my lawyer to call to get my money back.

sections.title: I: Rewrite with indirect object at the end
sections.instructions: Rewrite with the indirect object at the end of the sentence.

items.sentence: My dad got me a great computer game!
items.answer: My dad got a great computer game for me.

items.sentence: Dave sent Jill a really nice letter.
items.answer: Dave sent a really nice letter to Jill.

items.sentence: I threw Colin the ball.
items.answer: I threw the ball to Colin.

items.sentence: The waiter offered us a menu.
items.answer: The waiter offered a menu to us.

items.sentence: The hotel provides its guests with satellite television.
items.answer: The hotel provides satellite television to/for its guests.

items.sentence: My grandma taught me this song.
items.answer: My grandma taught this song to me.

sections.title: J: Cloze (Passive)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: It's hard for us to imagine what (1) ______ included in the European diet before America (2) ______ discovered (3) ______ Columbus in 1492.
So many ingredients which today (4) ______ grown all over the world (5) ______ unknown to medieval Europeans.
Potatoes, tomatoes, maize and chocolate (6) ______ all originally imported from the New World.
Until then, meals had (7) ______ prepared using ingredients native to Europe, such as root vegetables.
Of course, spices such as pepper (8) ______ been traded and added to food for centuries.
When the potato (9) ______ first introduced, it was surprisingly unpopular.
It (10) ______ considered to be poisonous and it took a long time to become common.
There is a story of Parmentier, a French army officer, who (11) ______ potatoes planted in the royal garden and (12) ______ Marie Antoinette to wear a potato flower to make them fashionable.
The poor peasants were curious about the new plants and many of them (13) ______ stolen to be planted in their own gardens. It was the start of French fries!
